compiled_state.title: clarity_check returns schema-violating {output,...} shape on missing ticket; siblings degrade properly
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
clarity_check returns a schema-violating {"output",...} dict on a missing ticket; sibling gates degrade with proper shapes; over MCP it surfaces as a pydantic ValidationError instead of a clean error.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD, temp store)
1. rebar.clarity_check("deadbeefdeadbeef") -> {"output": "ERROR: failed to retrieve ticket ...", "passed": false}
2. rebar.check_ac("deadbeefdeadbeef") -> {"verdict":"fail","criteria_count":0,"reason":"could not load ...","passed":false}
3. Over MCP: call_tool("clarity_check", ...) -> ToolError: 3 validation errors for ClarityResultOut (score/verdict/threshold missing).

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: one consistent missing-ticket contract across all three gates and across library/MCP (either RebarError like show_ticket, or a schema-conforming fail result).
- Actual: clarity_check alone violates clarity_result.schema.json and explodes the MCP output model.

## Source
- src/rebar/__init__.py:178-183 — fallback dict shape {"output", "passed"}.
- src/rebar/mcp_server.py:211-214 — ClarityResultOut rejects it.

## Fix direction
On engine exit 2 raise RebarError (consistent with show_ticket), or return {"score":0,"verdict":"fail","threshold":N} — pick ONE contract and apply to all three gates. See validation notes comment for the recommended option.

## Suggested RED test
All three gates on a nonexistent id, via library AND MCP; assert the single chosen contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] clarity_check missing-ticket result validates against clarity_result.schema.json OR raises RebarError — consistent with check_ac/quality_check after the change
- [ ] MCP clarity_check on missing id returns a clean ToolError or valid payload (no pydantic stack)
- [ ] RED test first; parity asserted across library + MCP

comments.body:
IMPLEMENTATION NOTES + RECOMMENDED CONTRACT (experimentally validated in sandbox, 2026-06-09):
RECOMMENDATION: contract (b) — structured failure dict, NOT raising. Reasons measured: siblings check_ac/quality_check return structured fail dicts on missing tickets (they never raise); (b) satisfies clarity_result.schema.json AND ClarityResultOut as-is; (a) would make clarity_check the only throwing gate and still surface as an opaque ToolError. Engine facts: clarity-check <missing> exits 2, empty stdout, stderr "ERROR: failed to retrieve ticket ..."; it also REJECTS --output json (library calls it bare — fine, it emits JSON by default).
Validated diff (src/rebar/__init__.py clarity_check):
    data = _json_or(cp.stdout, None)
    if not isinstance(data, dict) or "score" not in data:
        data = {"score": 0, "verdict": "fail", "threshold": 0, "reason": (cp.stderr or cp.stdout).strip()}
    data["passed"] = cp.returncode == 0
(threshold: 0 = "not evaluated" — do NOT hardcode 5; a config-overridden ticket_clarity.threshold would silently disagree.)
Validated end-to-end: library returns the dict; jsonschema-validates against clarity_result.schema.json; ClarityResultOut.model_validate accepts; MCP call_tool on missing id returns cleanly (no ToolError); existing-ticket path unchanged. Regression: test_mcp.py, test_surface.py, test_ws5d_quality_fileimpact.py, test_schema_outputs.py -> 34 passed.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

verify_commands.command: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/interfaces -k "clarity or check_ac or quality" -q
verify_commands.dd_text: all three gates share one missing-ticket contract via library and MCP
